summary: High‑energy Boston Garden show with scorching Half‑Step and classic Wheel→Wharf Rat
blurb: The May 7 1977 performance delivers a nonstop surge from the opener through a legendary second set. Highlights include a blistering Mississippi Half‑Step, a seamless The Wheel → Wharf Rat sequence, and an uplifting Terrapin Station.
review:
May 7, 1977 at Boston Garden is often cited as one of the peak moments of the ’77 spring run, and the recordings back that claim up to a hair’s breadth. Jerry Garcia rides on fire throughout, launching soaring solos on Mississippi Half‑Step, The Wheel and the extended Eyes Of The World → Wheel → Wharf Rat jam that many fans describe as “on fire” and “smoking.” Keith Godchaux’s piano and organ lock into the groove, especially on The Wheel and the Terrapin Station suite, while Phil Lesh provides a tight, beautiful bass foundation that shines on Half‑Step. The dual drummers, Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann, drive the band forward with solid power—only a minor quibble on the Jack Straw/Big River transition in one source—but otherwise keep the rhythm bursting with energy. Across all six recordings the setlist flows without dead spots: an energetic first set that builds to a peak with Half‑Step and Big River, then a second set that many reviewers call “one of the best ever,” highlighted by Terrapin Station, Estimated Prophet, Eyes Of The World and the iconic Wheel → Wharf Rat segue. The encore U.S. Blues caps a night that feels both tight and ecstatic, making this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

The crystal‑clear soundboard remaster (Bertha‑Ashley) captures every nuance of that magic, offering superb balance, loudness and clarity with only negligible glitches at the start. It stands out as the definitive source for experiencing the full power of this legendary night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are marked positive and repeatedly praise the same high‑point moments—Mississippi Half‑Step, The Wheel → Wharf Rat sequence, Terrapin Station and overall second‑set intensity—indicating strong consensus of exceptional performance.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard remaster with superb balance, loudness and detail; captures the full energy of the show without dropouts.

key_highlights: Blistering Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo that launches Jerry into peak form
Seamless The Wheel → Wharf Rat jam regarded as one of the era’s best segues
Terrapin Station suite opening a sublime second set
High‑energy first‑set flow ending with Big River and Tennessee Jed

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers soaring, fast‑paced solos—especially on Half‑Step, The Wheel and the Eyes → Wheel → Wharf Rat sequence—earning repeated praise for his tone and fire.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh stays locked in, providing a strong, tight bass line that shines on Half‑Step and underpins the entire set with beautiful phrasing.
band_performance.Keith: Godchaux’s piano/organ work is highlighted throughout, driving the groove on The Wheel, Terrapin Station and other jams; his playing is described as beautiful and perfectly placed.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart contributes solid, energetic drumming with only a minor critique on the Jack Straw/Big River transition; overall rhythm section bursts with power.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drumming matches Hart’s intensity, delivering tight foundations that fuel the high‑energy jams and seamless segues.
